title: Problems
summary: This page contains end-of-chapter problems from Chapter 1 related to signals and systems. Problems include determining power and RMS values for various sinusoidal and complex signals, analyzing periodic signals with DC offset, and working with the signum function. Several figures illustrate input-output signal pairs and a cubic signal waveform.

figures.caption: A periodic signal x(t) consisting of cubic segments (t³) that alternate between peaks of +8 and -8, repeating with period 4 over the time axis.
figures.description: The figure plots a periodic signal x(t) versus time t on horizontal and vertical axes, with repeated cubic-shaped segments labeled t^3. Over each interval from t=0 to 2 the curve rises from 0 to +8, then at t=2 it jumps discontinuously to -8 and increases back toward 0 by t=4; this pattern repeats every 4 units, with similar jumps shown at t=-2, 2, and 6. Students should notice the period T=4, the alternating peak levels +8 and -8, and the reset discontinuities between identical shifted cubic segments.
